evidence.text: Suspected sepsis due to pneumonia. Plan: continue antibiotics, monitor lactate and creatinine levels, and titrate vasopressors as needed. Nursing: patient tolerating oral intake as ordered. Nursing: skin warm and dry; no new rashes documented.

Patient was admitted with suspected sepsis due to pneumonia. He was treated with broad-spectrum antibiotics and vasopressors. Labs: lactate levels decreased to 1.8 mmol/L, creatinine levels decreased to 1.0 mg/dL. Vital signs: MAP 70 mmHg, temperature 36.8 C.

Vital signs: MAP 70 mmHg, temperature 36.8 C. Patient's condition improved significantly during his hospital stay. He was discharged home on oral antibiotics with follow-up appointments scheduled. Final diagnosis: sepsis due to pneumonia.

Patient remains febrile with a temperature of 38.2 C. Labs: lactate 2.8 mmol/L, creatinine 1.5 mg/dL, platelets 80 10^9/L. Vital signs: MAP 58 mmHg, temperature 38.2 C. Patient is being treated with continued antibiotics and vasopressors.

Patient is being treated with continued antibiotics and vasopressors. Plan: continue to monitor labs and vital signs, consider adding additional antibiotics if necessary. Patient's condition remains critical, but stable. Will continue to monitor for signs of organ dysfunction.

Patient was admitted with suspected sepsis due to pneumonia. He was treated with broad-spectrum antibiotics and vasopressors. Labs: lactate levels decreased to 1.8 mmol/L, creatinine levels decreased to 1.0 mg/dL. Vital signs: MAP 70 mmHg, temperature 36.8 C.

Labs: lactate 2.5 mmol/L, creatinine 1.2 mg/dL. Physical exam reveals bilateral lung crackles and decreased breath sounds. Patient is being treated with broad-spectrum antibiotics and vasopressors. Suspected sepsis due to pneumonia.

Patient remains febrile with a temperature of 38.2 C. Labs: lactate 2.8 mmol/L, creatinine 1.5 mg/dL, platelets 80 10^9/L. Vital signs: MAP 58 mmHg, temperature 38.2 C. Patient is being treated with continued antibiotics and vasopressors.

## Coding rationale
Clinical validation of billed Sepsis should rest on documented infection plus organ dysfunction indicators (sepsis3_summary, Section: Organ dysfunction indicators (simplified for demo)). Findings that affect payment grouping must be supported by chart indicators (icd10cm_guidelines_fy2026_summary, Section: Sepsis coding notes (high level)). This draft is for auditor review only and is not a payment decision. Key evidence IDs considered: E1, E10, E11, E12, E13, E14, E15, E16, E2, E3, E4, E5, E6, E7, E8, E9.

## Reviewer note
Machine-drafted aid generated from synthetic data. A qualified auditor makes the final determination.
